heading: Joint Requirements Oversight Council
label: 181 Joint Requirements Oversight Council
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (b). , which directed amendment of subsec. (b) “by amending paragraphs (1) through (7)”, was executed by generally amending pars. (1) to (7) and adding pars. (8) and (9), to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Prior to amendment, pars. (1) to (7) listed tasks in which the Joint Requirements Oversight Council was to assist the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(a)Subsec. (c)(1)(A). , struck out “or joint performance requirements” before period at end.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(b)Subsec. (d)(2). , inserted “strongly” before “consider” and struck out “paragraphs (1) and (2)” before “of subsection (b)”.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(c)(1)Subsec. (d)(3). , substituted “Input from service chiefs” for “Input from chiefs of staff” in heading and “and consider the views of the service chiefs in their roles as end users of capabilities delivered by the defense acquisition system” for “, and strongly consider, the views of the Chiefs of Staff of the armed forces, in their roles as customers of the acquisition system,” in text, and struck out “under subsection (b)(2) and joint performance requirements pursuant to subsection (b)(3)” before period at end.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(c)(2)Subsec. (d)(4). , substituted “and consider” for “, and strongly consider,”.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(c)(3)Subsec. (d)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(c)(4)Subsec. (e). , amended subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, text of subsec. (e) read as follows: “The Chief of Staff of an armed force is responsible for all performance requirements for that armed force and, except for performance requirements specified in subsections (b)(4) and (b)(5), such performance requirements do not need to be validated by the Joint Requirements Oversight Council.”Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(d)Subsec. (f). , inserted “and Engineering” after “Analytic” in heading and “and mission engineering activities” after “Office of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation” in text, and struck out “in operations research, systems analysis, and cost estimation” after “resources and expertise”.Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(e)Subsec. (h). , added par. (1), redesignated former par. (1) as (2), added pars. (1), (3), and (5), and struck out former pars. (2) and (3) which read as follows:Pub. L. 119–60, § 1811(f)“(2) The term ‘performance requirement’ means a performance attribute of a particular system considered critical or essential to the development of an effective military capability.“(3) The term ‘joint performance requirement’ means a performance requirement that is critical or essential to ensure interoperability or fulfill a capability gap of more than one armed force, Defense Agency, or other entity of the Department of Defense, or impacts the joint force in other ways such as logistics.”2024—Subsec. (c)(1)(F). substituted “in the grade of general” for “in the grade equivalent to the grade of general in the Army, Air Force, or Marine Corps, or admiral in the Navy”.Pub. L. 118–1592021—Subsec. (b)(2) to (5). , added par. (2) and redesignated former pars. (2) to (4) as (3) to (5), respectively. Former par. (5) redesignated (6).Pub. L. 117–81, § 903(a)(1)Subsec. (b)(6). , redesignated par. (5) as (6). Former par. (6) redesignated (7).Pub. L. 117–81, § 903(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(7). , which directed amendment of par. (6) by substituting “sections 4251(b), 4252(a)(4),” for “sections 2366a(b), 2366b(a)(4),”, was executed by making the substitution in par. (7) to reflect the probable intent of Congress and the redesignation of par. (6) as (7) by . See below.Pub. L. 117–81, § 1702(a)(2)Pub. L. 117–81, § 903(a)(1)(A), redesignated par. (6) as (7).Pub. (7) to reflect the probable intent of Congress and the redesignation of par. (6) as (7) by , effective . See above.Pub. L. 116–283, § 1850(m)Pub. Prior to amendment, section related to Joint Requirements Oversight Council and consisted of its establishment, mission, composition, advisors, organization, availability of oversight information to Congressional defense committees, and definitions.Pub. L. 114–3282015—Subsec. (d)(3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 114–922013—Subsec. (b)(1)(C). , substituted “in ensuring that appropriate trade-offs are made among life-cycle cost, schedule, and performance objectives, and procurement quantity objectives, in the establishment and approval of military requirements” for “in ensuring the consideration of trade-offs among cost, schedule, and performance objectives for joint military requirements”.Pub. L. 112–239, § 951(b)(1)Subsec. (b)(3). , substituted “the total cost of such resources” for “such resource level”.Pub. L. 112–239, § 951(b)(2)2011—Subsec. (a). , substituted “There is” for “The Secretary of Defense shall establish”.Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(d)Subsec. (b)(3). , which directed substitution of “Program Evaluation” for “Performance Evaluation”, could not be executed because of the amendment by . See below.Pub. L. 111–383, § 1075(b)(8)Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(c)(2), substituted “advisors to the Council under subsection (d)” for “Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller), the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics, and the Director of Cost Assessment and Performance Evaluation”.Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(c)(2)Subsec. (c)(1)(A). , inserted “Vice” before “Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff”.Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(a)(1)Subsec. (c)(1)(F). , added subpar. (F).Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(a)(2)Subsec. (c)(3). , struck out par. (3) which read as follows: “The functions of the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff as chairman of the Council may only be delegated to the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.”Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(a)(3)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “The following officials of the Department of Defense shall serve as advisors to the Council on matters within their authority and expertise:” for “The Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics, the Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller), and the Director of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation shall serve as advisors to the Council on matters within their authority and expertise.” and added subpars. (A) to (F).Pub. L. 111–383, § 841(c)(1)2009—Subsec. (b)(1)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 111–23, § 201(b)(1)Subsec. (b)(3). , inserted “, in consultation with the Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller), the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics, and the Director of Cost Assessment and Performance Evaluation,” after “assist the Chairman”.Pub. L. 111–23, § 201(b)(2)(A)Subsec. (b)(5). –(4), added par. (5).Pub. L. 111–23, § 201(b)(2)(B)Subsec. (d). , designated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) related to mission of Joint Requirements Oversight Council.Pub. L. 110–181, § 942(a)Subsec. (b)(4). substituted “section 2366a(b), section 2366b(a)(4),” for “section 2366a(a)(4), section 2366b(b),”.Pub. L. 110–417Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d). Former subsec. (d) redesignated (f).Pub. L. 110–181, § 942(b)(2)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 110–181, § 942(c)Subsec. (f). , redesignated subsec. (d) as (f).Pub. L. 110–181, § 942(b)(1)Subsec. (g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 110–181, § 942(d)2003—Subsec. (d)(2). substituted “subsection, the term ‘oversight” for “subsection:”, struck out “(A) The term ‘oversight” before “information’ means”, and struck out subpar. Effective Date of 2021 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 116–283section 1801(d) of Pub. L. 116–283section 3001 of this titleJan. 1, 2022

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title IX, § 905(b)110 Stat. 404Feb. 10, 1996“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall take effect on .”January 31, 1997

Input From Commanders of Combatant Commands, , , which provided that the Joint Requirements Oversight Council in the Department of Defense was to seek and consider input from the commanders of combatant commands, in accordance with , was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 111–23, title I, § 105(b)123 Stat. 1718section 181(d) of title 10Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XVIII, § 1811(h)(3)139 Stat. 1244May 22, 2009Dec. 18, 2025

Review of Joint Military Requirements, , , which provided that the Secretary of Defense was to ensure that each new joint military requirement recommended by the Joint Requirements Oversight Council was reviewed, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 111–23, title II, § 201(c)123 Stat. 1720Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XVIII, § 1811(h)(4)139 Stat. 1244May 22, 2009Dec. 18, 2025

Study Guidance for Analyses of Alternatives, , , which related to study guidance for analyses of alternatives for each joint military requirement for which the Chairman of the Joint Requirements Oversight Council was the validation authority, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 111–23, title II, § 201(d)123 Stat. 1720Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XVIII, § 1811(h)(4)139 Stat. 1244May 22, 2009Dec. 18, 2025, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–23, title II, § 201(d)123 Stat. 1720May 22, 2009“The Director of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation shall take the lead in the development of study guidance for an analysis of alternatives for each joint military requirement for which the Chairman of the Joint Requirements Oversight Council is the validation authority. In developing the guidance, the Director shall solicit the advice of appropriate officials within the Department of Defense and ensure that the guidance requires, at a minimum—“(1)full consideration of possible trade-offs among cost, schedule, and performance objectives for each alternative considered; and“(2)an assessment of whether or not the joint military requirement can be met in a manner that is consistent with the cost and schedule objectives recommended by the Joint Requirements Oversight Council.”

Deadlines for Inclusion of Core Mission References in Documents, , , which provided that, effective , all joint military requirements documents of the Joint Requirements Oversight Council produced to carry out its mission under were to reference the core mission areas organized and defined under former , was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 110–181, div. A, title IX, § 942(f)122 Stat. 288section 181(b)(1) of title 10section 118b of title 10Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XVIII, § 1811(h)(2)139 Stat. 1244Jan. 28, 2008June 1, 2009Dec. 18, 2025

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: There is a Joint Requirements Oversight Council in the Department of Defense.
evaluating global trends, emerging threats, and adversary capabilities to inform understanding of joint operational problems and to shape joint force design;
coordinating with commanders of combatant commands to compile, refine, and prioritize joint operational problems;

continuously reviewing and assessing joint military capabilities of elements of the Department of Defense listed in in a manner that meets applicable requirements in the national defense strategy under ;section 111(b) of this titlesection 113(g) of this title
identifying and prioritizing gaps and opportunities in joint military capabilities, including making recommendations for changes to address such capability and capacity gaps;
identifying advances in technology, innovative commercial solutions, and concepts of operation that could improve the military advantage of the joint force;

children.label: A
children.text: describe the joint operational problem to provide necessary context for the joint capability requirement;

children.label: B
children.text: proposes nonprescriptive solutions to joint operational problems; and

children.label: C
children.text: ensures system interoperability, where appropriate, between and among joint military capabilities;

children.label: A
children.text: addresses joint operational problems; and

children.label: B
children.text: evaluates force design initiatives of the Armed Forces to recommend acceptance, mitigation, or alternative force designs for the joint force;
maintaining a repository of joint operational problems and identification of capabilities to address those problems; and
evaluating effect of joint military capability requirements for the purposes of .section 4376(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, who is the Chair of the Council and is the principal adviser to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff for making recommendations about joint military capabilities.

children.label: B
children.text: An Army officer in the grade of general.

children.label: C
children.text: A Navy officer in the grade of admiral.

children.label: D
children.text: An Air Force officer in the grade of general.

children.label: E
children.text: A Marine Corps officer in the grade of general.

children.label: F
children.text: A Space Force officer in the grade of general.

children.heading: .—Selection of members
children.label: 2 .—Selection of members
children.text: Members of the Council under subparagraphs (B), (C), (D), and (E) of paragraph (1) shall be selected by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, after consultation with the Secretary of Defense, from officers in the grade of general or admiral, as the case may be, who are recommended for selection by the Secretary of the military department concerned.

children.heading: .—Recommendations
children.label: 3 .—Recommendations
children.text: In making any recommendation to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff as described in paragraph (1)(A), the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff shall provide the Chairman any dissenting view of members of the Council under paragraph (1) with respect to such recommendation.

children.label: A
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Policy.

children.label: B
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security.

children.label: C
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment.

children.label: i
children.text: shall provide assistance in evaluating the technical feasibility of requirements under development; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall identify options for expanding or generating new requirements based on opportunities provided by new or emerging technologies.

children.label: E
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller).

children.label: F
children.text: The Director of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation.

children.label: G
children.text: The Director of Operational Test and Evaluation.

children.label: H
children.text: The commander of a combatant command when matters related to the area of responsibility or functions of that command are under consideration by the Council.

children.heading: .—Input from combatant commands
children.label: 2 .—Input from combatant commands
children.text: The Council shall seek and strongly consider input from the commanders of the combatant commands in carrying out its mission under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Input from service chiefs
children.label: 3 .—Input from service chiefs
children.text: The Council shall seek and consider the views of the service chiefs in their roles as end users of capabilities delivered by the defense acquisition system on matters pertaining to a capability proposed by an armed force, Defense Agency, or other entity of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Input from vice chief of the national guard bureau
children.label: 4 .—Input from vice chief of the national guard bureau
children.text: The Council shall seek and consider the views of the Vice Chief of the National Guard Bureau regarding non-Federalized National Guard capabilities in support of homeland defense and civil support missions.

children.heading: .—Input from industry
children.label: 5 .—Input from industry
children.text: The Council shall seek views from private entities on commercially available technology to address joint operational problems or gaps in joint military capabilities.

children.heading: .—Responsibility for Capability Requirements
children.label: e .—Responsibility for Capability Requirements
children.text: Each service chief is responsible for the capability requirements of the armed force of such service chief.

children.heading: .—Analytic and Engineering Support
children.label: f .—Analytic and Engineering Support
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that analytical organizations within the Department of Defense, such as the Office of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation and mission engineering activities, provide resources and expertise to the Joint Requirements Oversight Council to assist the Council in performing the mission in subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Availability of Oversight Information to Congressional Defense Committees
children.label: g .—Availability of Oversight Information to Congressional Defense Committees
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that, in the case of a recommendation by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff to the Secretary that is approved by the Secretary, oversight information with respect to such recommendation that is produced as a result of the activities of the Joint Requirements Oversight Council is made available in a timely fashion to the congressional defense committees.
The term “joint capability requirement” means a capability that is critical or essential to address a joint operational problem.

The term “joint military capabilities” means the collective capabilities across the joint force, including both joint and force-specific capabilities, that are available to conduct military operations.

children.label: A
children.text: means a challenge across the joint force in achieving an assigned military objective based on current doctrine, emerging threats or future concepts; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include limitations in capabilities, capacity, resources, or the ability to effectively and efficiently coordinate across the joint force, with another combatant command, or among joint military capabilities.

The term “oversight information” means information and materials comprising analysis and justification that are prepared to support a recommendation that is made to, and approved by, the Secretary of Defense.
The term “service chief” has the meaning given in .section 3101 of this title
